basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.summary: First-year PhD candidate at HKUST NLP Group, supervised by Prof. Junxian He. Research focuses on natural language processing and machine learning, especially LLM reasoning, reinforcement learning, hallucination in vision-language models (VLM), and LLM truthfulness and interpretability.

work.summary: Research intern working on LLM reasoning and reinforcement learning.
Research intern at Tencent WeChat Group (WXG), advised by Zifei Shan. Worked on vision-language models.
Research intern at Shanghai AI Lab, advised by Prof. Yu Cheng. Worked on natural language processing.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST)

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: Synthesizes verifiable reasoning data at scale for learning logical reasoning and beyond, accepted at COLM 2026.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: Investigates the perception bottleneck of vision-language models for chart understanding.

publications.name: Optimizing Large Language Models for Contextual Reasoning in Multi-Task Environments
publications.summary: Proposes optimization strategies for LLMs to enhance contextual reasoning across multiple tasks, accepted at COAI 2026.

publications.name: Ipsum Lorem is all you need
publications.summary: Introduces a simplified approach to LLM architectures, achieving competitive performance with reduced computational costs. Accepted at COML 2026 (Oral).

publications.name: Ipsum Lorem is all you need for a workshop
publications.summary: Workshop version accepted at COML 2026 Workshop on Large Language Models (COMLW 2026).

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: Discovers a universal truthfulness hyperplane inside large language models, published at EMNLP 2024.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: Proposes using in-context sharpness as alerts for hallucination mitigation from an inner representation perspective, published at ICML 2024.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: Presents C-Eval, a multi-level multi-discipline Chinese evaluation suite for foundation models, published at NeurIPS 2023.

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: Proposes composing parameter-efficient modules with arithmetic operations, published at NeurIPS 2023.
